module1.clean_text:
Hospitalization report
The child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] years and [AGE] originally from and residing in Oran, followed since [DATE] for atypical severe combined immunodeficiency (Skid like), Hospitalized at our level on [DATE] for exacerbation of COPD. Family history:
Father: [AGE] years old, BES
Mother: [AGE] years old, BES, G3P2A1
Siblings:
A sister, aged [AGE] years, with a history of rickets, vitamin D dependent type 2. Screening for an innate immunity deficiency, negative.
A brother aged [AGE] screening for an innate immunity deficiency was negative. Consanguinity: 2nd degree
Deaths at a young age: 02 paternal uncles
Absence of chronic respiratory disease in the family
Low socio-economic level
Personal history:
Well-monitored pregnancy carried to term
Vaginal birth
No neonatal resuscitation, good APGAR
Birth weight: 2kg (low birth weight)
Meconium emitted less than 24 hours
Mixed breastfeeding, diversified at the age of [AGE] vaccinated but not documented, passport not returned
Anti-rickets prevention done
Good psychomotor development
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestic; -Consanguinity
-Death at a young age of [AGE] paternal uncles
-Chronic cough, recurrent respiratory infections
Clinic:
-Repeated respiratory infection
-Chronic respiratory failure: Clubbing, Thoracic deformation
-Delay in weight growth
Biology
- Immunodeficiency assessment done on [DATE] showing: an atypical severe combined immunodeficiency (leaky SCID). - Pan-hypogammaglobulinemia IgG: 0.29g/l IgA: 0.27g/l IgM: 0.18 g/l
- Profound T and B lymphopenia with virtual absence of naive T lymphocytes
Radiological:
-Thoracic CT [DATE] bilateral DDB with thoracic distension
Processing in progress:
Twice-daily physiotherapy + Na+ nebulization
O2 therapy at home
FORACORT spray 100 UG/6UG 01 puffs /2/d
Prophylactic antibiotic therapy:
Bactrim 01ddp et1/2/d
Itraconazol 100mg 01 tab/day
Zomax 01 DDP/ 1 day out of 2
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at SAP every 3 weeks
Last dose received: 10/22/2025 (0.8g/kg)
Intermediate history:
Consultation: No
Respiratory: Change in consistency and color of sputum
Treatment with Augmentin syrup 1DDP/08h
On the Hematological level: appearance of bruises on the lower limbs
HISTORY OF ILLNESS in relation to the reason for hospitalization: 11/18/2025
The onset of the signs dates back to a week earlier with an accentuation of the cough and a change in the consistency and color of the sputum with an increase in oxygen requirements and an accentuation of the signs of respiratory distress, hence his hospitalization. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 11/18/2025
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 12kg (-2.3 DS) Height: 107 CM (-2 DS). Moderate stature delay BMI: 10.5 kg/m (underweight)
P/A: 59%; P/T: 65% Moderate to severe malnutrition
General condition preserved, afebrile: T: 36.5° C, severe malnutrition, Absence of signs of dehydration
Skin exam:
Presence of bruising on the left lower limb
Healed burn lesions on both buttocks and left thigh
Pleuropulmonary examination: wet, polypneic cough, Fr: 44 c/min, SpO2: 95% under 3 Liters of O2
Signs of chronic respiratory failure: clubbing,
Thoracic deformity:
Respiratory distress caused by: Susternal, intercostal, subcostal pulling,
Pulmonary auscultation: diffuse wheezing and crackling rales in both pulmonary fields, more pronounced on the left
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 122 b/min, CRT < 3 sec, Auscultation: B1, B2 audible properties, absence of added noises
Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernial orifices, preserved transit
ENT: presence of ecchymotic lesions on the mucous membrane of the right cheek
Review of other devices: Is unremarkable
PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 11/18/25
NFS: GB: 4430 element, PNN: 2660; lymphocyte: 1020
HB: 9.5 g/dl; MCV: 93fl; CCMH: 34.2 [normocytic normochrome anemia]
PLQ: 30,000 el/mm 3.
CRP: 43.4 mg/l VS: 53 mm/h (1st hour) 96 mm/h (2nd hour)
PCT: 0.07ng/l
Ionogram: Na: 133.2mmol/l K: 3.84 mmol/l Cl: 104.1 mmol/l
Albumin: 39.9 g/l Creat: 4.66 mg/l Urea: 0.12 g/l Protein Total: 66.7 g/l
AST: 18.8 U/l ALT: 14.5 U/l gGT: 21.33 U/l
Ferritin: 37.32 ng/ml 25 OH Vit D: 50.4 ng/ml
support:
Hospitalization
Conditioning + peripheral venous route
Oxygen as needed
Immunoglobulin 0.8g/kg i.e. 10g
Bi Antibiotic Therapy: Tienam 100mg/kg/day (in 3 doses) Amikacin 15mg/kg
Nebulization: Astalin 0.5 cc+3.5 cc from SSI
Pulmicort 0.5mg 2 times/day
Respiratory physiotherapy
Dietary interview
Psychological interview. EVOLUTION:
On day 3 of hospitalization, the child presented with a dental abscess, a dental opinion was requested: child presents inflammation next to the right cheek, flagyl + Augmentin with follow-up appointment after 5 days
On day 4 of hospitalization, disappearance of bruises with a slight improvement in respiratory condition
On D8, dental advice: child presents a cavity which seems to have stopped and which does not require tooth extraction, with recommendation for oral care and cessation of treatment
Child stayed for 17 days having received Bi antibiotic therapy: Tienam for 17 days, amikacin for 6 days, with 3 courses of immunoglobulins close together given the infectious state:
1st: 11/18/2025: 0.8g/kg
2nd: 11/27/2025: 0.8g/kg
3rd: 04/12/2025: 0.4g/kg
Repercussions of the illness:
Nutritionally:
Failure to thrive: Weight: 12kg100 (-2.9ds) Height: 107 cm (-0.5ds). Moderate to severe malnutrition: Gomez: 61% Waterloo: 66%
Nutritional report: 11/18/2025 (correct)
Vitamin D3: 50.4ng/ml Ferritinemia: 37.32 ng/ml Albuminemia: 39.9ng/ml
On the infectious level:
*Skin: no cutaneous or mucosal mycoses and no skin infections. *ENT: dental abscess
*Pulmonary: pulmonary exacerbation of its DDB
*Digestive: absence of symptoms. On the cardiac level:
B1B2 audible, no added noise or hiss, HR: 98 b/min
Absence of signs of chronic cor pulmonale
Echoheart:
On the respiratory level:
Signs of chronic respiratory failure: Chest deformation with clubbing. Permanent bronchial congestion
Permanent mild respiratory distress requiring home oxygen therapy 1L/min
Gasometry 05/2025: severe CKD grade 2
pH: 7.235 PO2: 43.4 mmhg PCO2: 58.7mmhg
EFR 03/07/2025: restrictive syndrome with drop in FVC and FEV1, FEV1 value not increased by b2mimetic, BUT agitated child
CVF:
Chest CT:
On the digestive level:
Soft abdomen, preserved transit. Absence of organomegaly. Abdomino-pelvic ultrasound:
On the Hematological level:
Externalized cutaneous hemorrhagic syndrome
FNS: NFS: GB: 4430 item, PNN: 2660; lymphocyte: 1020
HB: 9.5 g/dl; MCV: 93fl; CCMH: 34.2 [normocytic normochrome anemia]
PLQ: 30,000 el/mm 3. Peripheral blood smear: 08/19/2025
Myelogram:
Hemato opinion made on 02/09/2025: Richness 1 to 2 with indication BOM TO DISCUSS IF (It is a congenital amegakaryocyte or beginning of bone marrow aplasia with indication of karyotype) > SUSPICION OF FANCONI. Karyotype result: Inconclusive
Residual IgG rate 09/28/2025: 9.45g/ml
CONCLUSION:
The child Kherissi Maram, aged 06 and a half years old, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed since the age of 5 years for severe atypical combined PID “Skid Like” with COPD, DDB, Chronic Respiratory Failure, Thrombocytopenia. Readmitted for moderate exacerbation of his DDB. Child discharged on 04/12/2025 after good clinical and biological progress, and discharge weight: 12.3kg
To collect / To do:
Inspection appointment on: 12/18/2025
Plan a request for residual IgG levels after the end of the next treatment
Resident of the department: Dr Djoudi Assistant of the department: Dr OUSSALAH

test.expanded_text: History, EKG, Age, Risk factors, initial Troponin

ordo_candidates.name: MHC class II deficiency
ordo_candidates.name_fr: Déficit en molécules HLA de classe II

phenopacket.input:
Hospitalization report
The child Kherissi Maram, aged 06 years and 07 months, originally from and residing in Oran, followed since 03/2024 for atypical severe combined immunodeficiency (Skid like), Hospitalized at our level on 11/18/2025 for exacerbation of COPD. Family history:
Father: 37 years old, BES
Mother: 36 years old, BES, G3P2A1
Siblings:
A sister, aged 08 years, with a history of rickets, vitamin D dependent type 2. Screening for an innate immunity deficiency, negative.
A brother aged 06 months, screening for an innate immunity deficiency was negative. Consanguinity: 2nd degree
Deaths at a young age: 02 paternal uncles
Absence of chronic respiratory disease in the family
Low socio-economic level
Personal history:
Well-monitored pregnancy carried to term
Vaginal birth
No neonatal resuscitation, good APGAR
Birth weight: 2kg (low birth weight)
Meconium emitted less than 24 hours
Mixed breastfeeding, diversified at the age of 4 months
Correctly vaccinated but not documented, passport not returned
Anti-rickets prevention done
Good psychomotor development
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestic; -Consanguinity
-Death at a young age of 02 paternal uncles
-Chronic cough, recurrent respiratory infections
Clinic:
-Repeated respiratory infection
-Chronic respiratory failure: Clubbing, Thoracic deformation
-Delay in weight growth
Biology
- Immunodeficiency assessment done on 03/13/2024, showing: an atypical severe combined immunodeficiency (leaky SCID). - Pan-hypogammaglobulinemia IgG: 0.29g/l IgA: 0.27g/l IgM: 0.18 g/l
- Profound T and B lymphopenia with virtual absence of naive T lymphocytes
Radiological:
-Thoracic CT 11/2024: bilateral DDB with thoracic distension
Processing in progress:
Twice-daily physiotherapy + Na+ nebulization
O2 therapy at home
FORACORT spray 100 UG/6UG 01 puffs /2/d
Prophylactic antibiotic therapy:
Bactrim 01ddp et1/2/d
Itraconazol 100mg 01 tab/day
Zomax 01 DDP/ 1 day out of 2
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at SAP every 3 weeks
Last dose received: 10/22/2025 (0.8g/kg)
Intermediate history:
Consultation: No
Respiratory: Change in consistency and color of sputum
Treatment with Augmentin syrup 1DDP/08h
On the Hematological level: appearance of bruises on the lower limbs
HISTORY OF ILLNESS in relation to the reason for hospitalization: 11/18/2025
The onset of the signs dates back to a week earlier with an accentuation of the cough and a change in the consistency and color of the sputum with an increase in oxygen requirements and an accentuation of the signs of respiratory distress, hence his hospitalization. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 11/18/2025
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 12kg (-2.3 DS) Height: 107 CM (-2 DS). Moderate stature delay BMI: 10.5 kg/m (underweight)
P/A: 59%; P/T: 65% Moderate to severe malnutrition
General condition preserved, afebrile: T: 36.5° C, severe malnutrition, Absence of signs of dehydration

Presence of bruising on the left lower limb
Healed burn lesions on both buttocks and left thigh
Pleuropulmonary examination: wet, polypneic cough, Fr: 44 c/min, SpO2: 95% under 3 Liters of O2
Signs of chronic respiratory failure: clubbing,
Thoracic deformity:
Respiratory distress caused by: Susternal, intercostal, subcostal pulling,
Pulmonary auscultation: diffuse wheezing and crackling rales in both pulmonary fields, more pronounced on the left
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 122 b/min, CRT < 3 sec, Auscultation: B1, B2 audible properties, absence of added noises
Abdominopelvic examination: Soft abdomen, no organomegaly, free hernial orifices, preserved transit
ENT: presence of ecchymotic lesions on the mucous membrane of the right cheek
Review of other devices: Is unremarkable
PARACLINICAL ASSESSMENT UPON ADMISSION: 11/18/25
NFS: GB: 4430 element, PNN: 2660; lymphocyte: 1020
HB: 9.5 g/dl; MCV: 93fl; CCMH: 34.2 [normocytic normochrome anemia]
PLQ: 30,000 el/mm 3.
CRP: 43.4 mg/l VS: 53 mm/h (1st hour) 96 mm/h (2nd hour)
PCT: 0.07ng/l
Ionogram: Na: 133.2mmol/l K: 3.84 mmol/l Cl: 104.1 mmol/l
Albumin: 39.9 g/l Creat: 4.66 mg/l Urea: 0.12 g/l Protein Total: 66.7 g/l
AST: 18.8 U/l ALT: 14.5 U/l gGT: 21.33 U/l
Ferritin: 37.32 ng/ml 25 OH Vit D: 50.4 ng/ml
support:

Conditioning + peripheral venous route
Oxygen as needed
Immunoglobulin 0.8g/kg i.e. 10g
Bi Antibiotic Therapy: Tienam 100mg/kg/day (in 3 doses) Amikacin 15mg/kg
Nebulization: Astalin 0.5 cc+3.5 cc from SSI
Pulmicort 0.5mg 2 times/day

Hemato opinion made on 02/09/2025: Richness 1 to 2 with indication BOM TO DISCUSS IF (It is a congenital amegakaryocyte or beginning of bone marrow aplasia with indication of karyotype) > SUSPICION OF FANCONI. Karyotype result: Inconclusive
Residual IgG rate 09/28/2025: 9.45g/ml

The child Kherissi Maram, aged 06 and a half years old, originally from and residing in Oran, from a non-consanguineous couple, followed since the age of 5 years for severe atypical combined PID “Skid Like” with COPD, DDB, Chronic Respiratory Failure, Thrombocytopenia.
